title: Should a topic be able to leave `Prepared` (un-prepare / defer-from-Prepared) before it is scheduled?
question: Should a topic be able to leave `Prepared` (un-prepare / defer-from-Prepared) before it is scheduled?
resolution: Default adopted (2026-07-09, D-15 Tier 3): no un-prepare transition in v1 — `DeferTopic` allows Triage/Accepted/Scheduled/InCommittee but not Prepared, so a mis-prepared topic stays Prepared until it is scheduled (then it can be Deferred). Accept the gap; add a Prepared→Accepted un-prepare only if operators hit stuck-Prepared topics in practice

title: Should non-topic-scoped governance artifacts (ADR, Invariant, ResearchMission) support owner-based allow-if-owner (AiO), or is A-only (Chairman/Secretary) the accepted behavior?
question: Should non-topic-scoped governance artifacts (ADR, Invariant, ResearchMission) support owner-based allow-if-owner (AiO), or is A-only (Chairman/Secretary) the accepted behavior?
resolution: Default (2026-07-13, from P15a): accept **A-only**. The matrix marks `Research.Manage` (#26), ADR (#18) and Invariant (#21) AiO for Member/Reviewer, but the shared `CapabilityHandler` resolves AiO only for an `ITopicScopedResource` (a per-topic relationship). These aggregates are not topic-scoped, so Member/Reviewer are denied create/manage — consistent across ADR/Invariant/Research; `OwnerUserId` is attribution, not authz. Revisit only if the committee needs Member-owned governance authoring; realizing it is a **cross-cutting authz change** (a resource/owner ABAC handler affecting all three), not a per-module fix

title: `Template.TargetType` value set — FR-119 `{Topic, ADR, MoM, Research Mission}` vs domain-model §403 sketch `{Topic, ADR, MoM, Document, Action}`?
question: `Template.TargetType` value set — FR-119 `{Topic, ADR, MoM, Research Mission}` vs domain-model §403 sketch `{Topic, ADR, MoM, Document, Action}`?
resolution: Resolved (2026-07-14, P15d-2): adopt **FR-119's set** `{Topic, Adr, MinutesOfMeeting, ResearchMission}` — the functional requirement is the contract (OWASP LLM01), and §403 is an illustrative sketch that even contradicts itself (§404 omitted Action, neither line listed ResearchMission). Document/Action have no FR → YAGNI; the enum serializes as its string name (int-stored, no CHECK) so any value is addable later with **no migration**. domain-model §402-404 reconciled in the same PR; member spelling mirrors Traceability `ArtifactType`
custom_attributes: {"v1": {"ID": "OQ-051", "Question": "`Template.TargetType` value set — FR-119 `{Topic, ADR, MoM, Research Mission}` vs domain-model §403 sketch `{Topic, ADR, MoM, Document, Action}`?", "Blocking?": "No", "Resolution": "Resolved (2026-07-14, P15d-2): adopt **FR-119's set** `{Topic, Adr, MinutesOfMeeting, ResearchMission}` — the functional requirement is the contract (OWASP LLM01), and §403 is an illustrative sketch that even contradicts itself (§404 omitted Action, neither line listed ResearchMission). Document/Action have no FR → YAGNI; the enum serializes as its string name (int-stored, no CHECK) so any value is addable later with **no migration**. domain-model §402-404 reconciled in the same PR; member spelling mirrors Traceability `ArtifactType`", "Status": "Approved"}}

title: Should Draft/Archived wiki pages appear in the tree for all readers, or be filtered by role/status?
question: Should Draft/Archived wiki pages appear in the tree for all readers, or be filtered by role/status?
resolution: Default (2026-07-14, P15e): the wiki backend returns every status to every reader (reads are committee-wide), but the design draws no distinction. Adopted: tree shows **Published to everyone**, **Draft only to managers** (Chairman/Secretary, dot-marked), **Archived excluded** from the tree. A selected page still opens by direct key regardless. Revisit if members need to see drafts or browse archived pages

title: AC-012 presumes the action owner *attempts* to verify and is blocked; the UI instead **prevents** the attempt (hides Verify from owner/completer). Is prevention an acceptable satisfaction of the SoD-1
question: AC-012 presumes the action owner *attempts* to verify and is blocked; the UI instead **prevents** the attempt (hides Verify from owner/completer). Is prevention an acceptable satisfaction of the SoD-1 criterion?
resolution: Interpretation adopted (2026-07-17, P17b): **yes** — SoD-1 is satisfied by prevention. The owner never sees Verify (proven live, p17b-immutability.spec.ts) and the API 403 is the backstop (ActionsApiTests). AC-012 → Met with this note. Revisit only if an explicit owner-attempt-then-403 UX is required

title: AC-022 presumes a second cast is rejected in-UI with "You have already voted"; the SPA instead routes a re-vote to `/change` (Fork 1, editable-until-close), so that rejection **never occurs in-product
question: AC-022 presumes a second cast is rejected in-UI with "You have already voted"; the SPA instead routes a re-vote to `/change` (Fork 1, editable-until-close), so that rejection **never occurs in-product**. Is the divergence accepted?
resolution: Recorded as a product↔criterion divergence (2026-07-17, P17b, operator-ruled): the one-ballot invariant holds via the API 409 + DB unique index (VotesApiTests); the in-UI experience is edit-in-place, not rejection (proven live). AC-022 → Met with the divergence foregrounded. **The audit note must NOT claim the UI rejects a second ballot — it does not.** Revisit if a hard in-UI second-cast block is ever required

title: AC-025's tally and chair-action legs have no mutating endpoint (only the ballot leg does). Is immutable-by-absence an acceptable satisfaction for those legs?
question: AC-025's tally and chair-action legs have no mutating endpoint (only the ballot leg does). Is immutable-by-absence an acceptable satisfaction for those legs?
resolution: Interpretation adopted (2026-07-17, P17b): **yes** — the ballot leg is proven live (POST `/change` on a closed vote → 409, p17b-immutability.spec.ts); the tally + chair-action legs are immutable because no endpoint mutates a closed vote's frozen tally or its counter of record. title: The design splits decision issuance across two surfaces — the "Record a decision" create form (ACMP Create Flows & Dialogs) and the closed-vote "Record override" control (ACMP Decision, Voting & ADR)
question: The design splits decision issuance across two surfaces — the "Record a decision" create form (ACMP Create Flows & Dialogs) and the closed-vote "Record override" control (ACMP Decision, Voting & ADR) — but the shipped `RecordDecisionDialog` merges record + issue into ONE meeting-workspace dialog, omits the design create-form's Approving authority / Effective date / Affected systems (no backend field — the chair is attributed at issue, `IssuedAt` is server-set), adds Title/Statement/Alternatives (backend-required, present on the read page but abbreviated out of the create form), and folds the separate immutability confirm into an inline warning. Is the reconciliation accepted, or is a two-surface build owed?
resolution: Reconciliation adopted (2026-07-18, P17b): **one dialog** — in-meeting the committee records + ratifies in one place, so record→issue is a single chairman-gated dialog launched from the agenda item (D-15 show-and-enforce; the SoD-3 403 surfaces inline). Design-only fields dropped rather than inventing backend fields (implement-as-specified, OWASP LLM01); the filled form + "Record & issue" primary is the deliberate immutability confirm. AC-015/016 + the F-03 leg proven live (p17b-decision-issue.spec.ts). A design-doc update to a single record→issue dialog is owed. Revisit only if operators need a standalone Draft-only record form for the secretary

title: Production SQL Server edition — Express (free, 10 GB/DB) vs a licensed Standard edition
question: Should production run SQL Server Express (free production licence, 10 GB per database) or a licensed Standard edition? Express limits are all satisfied by this workload today, but the 10 GB/DB ceiling binds as audit/Hangfire history grows.
resolution: Recommended default: Express in both environments. Revisit only if sys.database_files approaches the cap; a licence would lift it. Flagged, not decided (operator Q4).

title: Region — us-east-1 (cheapest) vs me-central-1 (lower Gulf latency)
question: Host in us-east-1 (cheapest, verified $0.0416/hr) or a Middle East region (me-central-1/me-south-1 at $0.0502/hr, +20.7%) for lower latency to Gulf users?
resolution: Recommended default: us-east-1 for a <=20-user internal tool; ~150-250 ms RTT is acceptable. Flagged, not decided.

title: Runtime OIDC config injection to collapse the two per-environment web images into one
question: Should the SPA read VITE_OIDC_AUTHORITY at runtime (config injection) instead of at build time, so a single web image serves both environments?
resolution: Recommended default: defer. It is a source change; two build-tagged images (web:<sha>-uat|prod) are the smaller step now. Flagged.

title: Enable Webex in the UAT environment at all
question: Should UAT enable Webex (its own space + bot), or keep it off until a dedicated UAT space exists?
resolution: Recommended default: off in UAT until a separate UAT Webex space + bot exist — never let UAT post into the committee's production space. AC-071 keeps in-app the sole required channel. Flagged (operator Q5).

title: Merge the worker container into the api to drop one more container
question: Should the dedicated Hangfire worker be merged back into the api process to save a container on the constrained host?
resolution: Recommended default: keep them split — ADR-0024 separated them deliberately; merging needs an ADR-0024 amendment and re-introduces the coupling it removed. Flagged.
